name: CRUD/read
description: Conformance tests for resource read (GET /{type}/{id}) semantics: successful read returns matching metadata, ETag, and security headers; reading a never-existing id returns 404; reading a deleted resource returns 410 (or 404). Fixture tagged identifier READ-BASIC-1 (fhir262) to avoid colliding with other suites on a shared test server.

variable.name: obsId
variable.description: The server-assigned id from the create response

variable.name: obsVersionId
variable.description: The server-assigned version id from the create response

variable.name: obsLastUpdated
variable.description: The server-assigned lastUpdated timestamp from the create response

operation.description: Create an Observation to obtain a server-assigned id/version for the read tests

test.name: read returns the resource with matching metadata and headers
test.description: GET /Observation/{id} must return HTTP 200 with the same id, meta.versionId, and meta.lastUpdated as the create response, an ETag header reflecting the current version, and the X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff security header (FHIR R4 §3.2.0.1.1).

operation.description: Read the Observation by server-assigned id

assert.description: Response body must be an Observation
Returned id must match the one assigned at create
Returned meta.versionId must match the one assigned at create
Returned meta.lastUpdated must match the one assigned at create
ETag header must reflect the current version
X-Content-Type-Options security header must be present and set to nosniff

test.name: read of a never-existing id returns 404
test.description: GET /Observation/{id} for an id that was never assigned by the server must return 404 Not Found.

operation.description: Attempt to read an id that was never created

test.name: read after delete returns 410 or 404
test.description: GET /Observation/{id} after DELETE must return 410 (Gone) or 404 (Not Found). FHIR R4 specifies 410 Gone for a resource that existed and was deleted, so that is the primary assertion. 404 is accepted as a warningOnly alternative for servers that do not distinguish deleted from never-existed.

operation.description: Delete the Observation created in setup

assert.description: Delete must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Attempt to read the deleted Observation

assert.description: Primary: expect 410 Gone (FHIR R4 behavior for a deleted resource)
Alternative: 404 Not Found is also accepted (warningOnly so 404-returning servers still pass this suite)

operation.description: Teardown: attempt to clean up the Observation (may already be deleted by the delete test — that is expected)
